title: Incoming webhook? (i.e. adding an entry to a board based from a 3rd party app)?
excerpt:
I’m using intercom for a chat bot in my website, and I want to add an entry into a board in Monday when a certain action takes place on the chat. 
Intercom allows me to ping a webhook address, but on the Monday.com help …

posts.author: whatevs (Adam)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m using intercom for a chat bot in my website, and I want to add an entry into a board in Monday when a certain action takes place on the chat.

Intercom allows me to ping a webhook address, but on the Monday.com help docs (https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360003540679-Webhook-Integration-), I could only find information about how to set up an outgoing webhook (i.e. when something happens in Monday, send a webhook to another app).

How can I achieve this?

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @whatevs
Welcome to the community. Monday.com does not offer incoming webhooks. What you can do is write your own integration app and listen to webhooks from an external source and issue GraphQL API call to make updates in monday boards.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@whatevs,

Integromat (and I assume Zapier) is another alternative.

posts.author: andrewalmand (Andrew Almand)
posts.cookedHtml:
@whatevs

See documentation from Integromat below (assuming this is the correct Intercom app):

Make

Intercom Integration | Workflow Automation | Make

Connect Intercom to sync data between apps and create powerful automated workflows. Integrate over 1000 apps on Make.

Integromat

Intercom App Help Docs | Integromat Help Center

The Intercom modules allow you to watch, create, update, list, and/or delete admins, companies, conversations, events, leads, notes, segments, tags,

You would start you scenario with a webhook from Intercom, and then build out the Monday portion with data retrieved from Intercom via the webhook.

Integromat

Webhooks App Help Docs | Integromat Help Center

Custom Webhook To call a third party webhook (outgoing webhooks) use one of the HTTP modules. Any application that is connected to the Internet and a

Hope this helps!

posts.author: whatevs (Adam)
posts.cookedHtml: Obviously went over all of those before asking, I couldn’t really understand :\ Thanks

posts.author: beverlyRoadGoose (Tobi)
posts.cookedHtml: If this is still relevant, HookPanda offers this functionality and might be what you need. If you would like to check it out: https://hookpanda.io
